_comment: Language-neutral conformance vectors. Defined by the protocol, NOT by any one implementation: every conformant emitter/verifier MUST reproduce them. The canonicalization vectors are the interoperability crux - the signed bytes must be byte-identical across languages or signatures will not verify across stacks. See SPEC.md, section Canonicalization.

canonicalization._note: Non-ASCII KEY sort is by UTF-16 code unit (RFC 8785 / JCS), NOT code point or UTF-8 byte: the emoji's lead surrogate D83D sorts BEFORE FB33. Reachable in-domain via constitution.parameters / provenance record keys. A port that sorts keys by Unicode code point (naive Python sorted) or UTF-8 byte (Go/Rust string <) produces the reverse order, and its signatures will not verify across stacks.

Decimal scalars (e.g. redTeam.score, mandate ratios) serialize per ECMAScript Number::toString, which JCS adopts. Integers and short decimals only — ADP carries no exponential-range doubles, so no Ryu corner cases are tested here.
